reasoning: The Toronto Blue Jays are off to a mediocre start in 2025, with an 18-20 record going into a May 9 series where they won a couple of games. Preseason and early season futures odds consistently place them around +5000 to +6000 to win the World Series (translating to a true probability slightly below 2%). They play in arguably baseball's toughest division, and multiple models project them in the 78-84 win range, which requires a strong second half just to qualify for a Wild Card spot. Given their unimpressive start and the May 1 Polymarket price of 2%, there is no reason to meaningfully upgrade their chances from the baseline ~2% projection.
